title: The Seventh Sunday after the Epiphany
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## February 20, 2022 | Preacher: Richard Lawson

---

LITURGICAL CONTEXT

February 20, 2022 was the Seventh Sunday after the Epiphany in the Christian calendar, falling within Ordinary Time before the approach of Lent (which began on Ash Wednesday, March 2, 2022). The Revised Common Lectionary readings for this Sunday were likely 2 Kings 2:1-12 (Elijah's ascension), Psalm 50:1-6, 2 Corinthians 4:3-6, and Mark 9:2-9 (the Transfiguration account). This Sunday's readings emphasized divine revelation and the transfiguration of Christ, themes central to the post-Epiphany season focused on manifestations of Christ's divinity.

EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in the midst of preparations for the 80th General Convention, scheduled for July 2022 in Baltimore. In early 2022, denomination-wide discussions were intensifying around LGBTQ+ inclusion policies and canonical reforms that would be debated at Convention. The Diocese of Colorado was led by Bishop Kee Sloan (installed in 2021), who had begun his tenure during a period of post-pandemic reorganization of parish life and finances.

DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver had experienced a severe winter storm in mid-February (around February 15-16) that dumped significant snow on the Front Range and created hazardous travel conditions throughout the region. The Colorado legislature was in session in Denver during this period, with ongoing debates about education funding, criminal justice reform, and response to inflation. Downtown Denver and the Capitol Hill neighborhood near Saint John's Cathedral were slowly reopening indoor activities as COVID-19 restrictions were being relaxed throughout Colorado.

NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The dominant national story was Russia's invasion of Ukraine on February 24, 2022—four days after this sermon—so the week of February 14-20 was marked by escalating war warnings and emergency diplomacy as President Biden and European leaders attempted last-minute negotiations to prevent the invasion. Within the US, the Justice Department was actively investigating the January 6, 2021 Capitol riot with multiple indictments and hearings. The midterm elections were nine months away, with inflation at 7.5% (the highest in 40 years) becoming a central political concern affecting household budgets nationwide.

INTERNATIONAL NEWS

NATO was on high alert with troop movements and emergency summits as Western intelligence confirmed Russian military buildup on the Ukraine border, with invasion expected imminently (it occurred four days after this sermon). The Olympic Games in Beijing had just concluded on February 20, 2022, with China's COVID-zero policies and human rights concerns dominating international discourse. The World Health Organization was still managing the global COVID-19 pandemic, though vaccination rates were higher and restrictions were easing in most Western nations.

CULTURAL MOMENT

America was in a transitional mood: pandemic fatigue was pronounced, economic anxiety was rising due to inflation, and geopolitical tensions were creating uncertainty about global stability. The upcoming Russian invasion of Ukraine would fundamentally shift the cultural and political moment within days of this sermon. Within Denver specifically, there was a sense of cautious reopening—gatherings were resuming, but uncertainty about the future remained. Sports culture was active (the Denver Broncos' NFL season had ended; college basketball was underway), and the Winter Olympics had just concluded, providing a brief respite from domestic political tensions.
